title: Matthew Cumming
content:
Co-Leader, National Business Law Group | Co-Head, National Private Equity Group | Managing Partner, New York Office
People.ContactEmail mcumming@mccarthy.ca
- Cambridge University
- University of Toronto
Known for his clarity of judgment and cross‑border perspective, Matthew helps boards and investors make critical decisions on complex Canadian M&A transactions, delivering strategic, commercially grounded advice when stakes are highest.
Matthew is Co-Leader of our National Business Law Group, Managing Partner of our New York office, Co-Head of our National Private Equity Group and a Partner in our Mergers and Acquisitions Group. Based in New York, he brings a unique vantage point to Canadian and international clients navigating complex M&A and private equity transactions. He advises clients on investments, contested transactions and acquisitions across various key industries, including infrastructure, technology, mining, REITs and consumer markets. With two decades of experience at the intersection of Canadian and U.S. dealmaking, he helps clients anticipate regulatory, governance and execution challenges and align their strategic objectives across jurisdictions.
Clients turn to Matthew for his ability to simplify complexity, manage competing stakeholder interests, and drive transactions to completion. Known for his strategic leadership, creativity, keen business sense, and pragmatic approach, he is a valued advisor to pension plans, private equity sponsors, global corporates and high‑growth companies.
Matthew is recognized by Chambers Global as the only U.S.-based lawyer ranked for Private Equity in Canada, which reflects his longstanding leadership in the Canadian and cross‑border private equity market.

#### Representative public transactions include the following:
- OMERS in connection with its acquisition of an indirect stake in Maple Leaf Sports & Entertainment for US$400 million
- Ontario Teachers’ Pension Plan, Canada Pension Plan Investment Board, Caisse de dépôt et placement du Québec and Alberta Investment Management Corporation, in connection with Maple Group’s $3.8-billion acquisition of each of TMX Group, CDS and Alpha, resulting in the creation of an integrated clearing and exchange group in Canada
- Slate Asset Management L.P. in connection with its “GP stakes” sale of a minority non-voting interest to Goldman Sachs’ Petershill Program
- Cameco Corporation in connection with its acquisition of 49% of Westinghouse Electric Company, one of the world’s largest nuclear services businesses, from Brookfield Business Partners in a deal valued at US$7.9 billion
- OMERS Infrastructure in its $3.25 billion offer to acquire an equity interest in 407 International Ltd., which holds the world’s first all-electronic, open-access toll highway
- Cathay Capital in connection with its acquisition of Moose Knuckles, a Canadian luxury outerwear brand, and the subsequent strategic investment in Moose Knuckles from Bosideng
- Canadian counsel to Apollo Global Management in connection with its acquisition of The Travel Corporation
- Slate Asset Management in connection with its going-private acquisition of Huntingdon Capital Corp.; its US$2.33 billion portfolio and platform acquisition from Annaly Capital Management; and its C$1.14 billion acquisition from Cominar REIT
- A large technology company in connection with its contested topping offer for Converge Technologies, a TSX-listed company, following Converge’s arrangement agreement with H.I.G. Capital
- Oil Search in connection with its US$2.2 billion offer for, and takeover battle with Exxon Mobil over, InterOil, a NYSE-listed oil and gas company
- Royal Gold, Inc. in its contested $749 million acquisition of International Royalty Corporation by plan of arrangement
- Otsuka Pharmaceutical, a Japanese pharmaceutical company, in connection with its going-private acquisition of Mindset Pharma
- Victor Capital in connection with its acquisition of The Coats Company
- Allkem in its $10.6 billion merger with Livent
- Orocobre Limited in connection with its $4 billion merger with Galaxy Resources
- Sun Life Financial in its acquisition of Bentall Kennedy Group, one of North America's largest real estate investment advisors and providers of real estate services
- OMERS Infrastructure in its $2 billion unsolicited take-over bid for Teranet Income Fund, a leader in the delivery and transformation of statutory registry services
- A joint venture of Marfrig Global Foods and Archer Daniels Midland in connection with its going-private acquisition of a TSX-listed company
- Investment Management Corporation of Ontario in connection with numerous private equity investments
- Manulife Capital in connection with multiple private equity investments
- Consortium of international Private Equity funds in connection with their acquisition of a majority interest in a privately-held Canadian retail business
- European Private Equity fund and family office in connection with their acquisition of a majority interest in a privately-held Canadian agribusiness
- CEF Holding’s acquisition (together with a consortium of other private equity partners) of IAMGOLD’s Niobec Mine
- Nieuport Aviation Infrastructure, as consortium lead, in connection with the consortium arrangements for the acquisition of the Billy Bishop Toronto City Airport passenger terminal from Porter Aviation
- OMERS Infrastructure in connection with its investment in an independent power producer in Ontario
- Newfoundland Capital Corporation in connection with its acquisition by Stingray Digital Group
- Computacenter plc in connection with its acquisition by plan of arrangement of TSX-listed Pivot Technology Solutions
- Orocobre Limited in connection with its acquisition of Advantage Lithium by way of plan of arrangement
- Slate Retail REIT in its going-private acquisition of Slate U.S. Opportunity (No.3) Realty Trust
- BioSteel Sports Nutrition Inc. in its sale of a majority stake to Canopy Growth Corp.
- ATS Andlauer Income Fund in connection with the unsolicited insider take-over bid by Andlauer Management Group
Matthew is the co‑author of Shareholder Remedies in Canada , a leading text on shareholder rights and corporate governance. He previously served as an adjunct professor at the University of Toronto Faculty of Law, where he taught shareholder rights and activism, and he is a frequent speaker at industry conferences on governance, cross‑border M&A, and private equity trends.
Prior to joining the Firm, Matthew clerked for the Honourable Mr. Justice John C. Major at the Supreme Court of Canada, and previously practised corporate and securities law at a leading New York-based international law firm. He holds a BA in Economics from Yale University, a JD from the University of Toronto (Bronze Medallist), and an LLM in Commercial Law from Cambridge University (First Class Honours).
